input.title: Insurance Management Platform
input.description: Insurance Management Platform is a web-based enterprise platform that centralizes operational workflows, data visibility, and cross-functional reporting.

user_classes.Admin: Configures the system, manages users, and oversees permissions and settings.
user_classes.Manager: Oversees departmental workflows, approvals, KPIs, and operational reporting.
user_classes.Employee: Performs day-to-day operational tasks within assigned modules and permissions.
user_classes.User: Generic authenticated platform user with access limited by role and department.

definitions.Admin: System user with full administrative privileges.
definitions.Manager: Operational owner of a department, module, or workflow.
definitions.Employee: End user performing role-specific business operations.

constraints: Initial release will focus on the web platform only
Role-based access control is mandatory across all modules
Operational data must be auditable and retained according to policy

operating_environment.frontend: Web-based interface built with React and accessible through modern browsers.
operating_environment.backend: Service layer implemented with Java Spring Boot, Oracle DB, RabbitMQ for business operations.
operating_environment.deployment: Cloud-hosted environment with centralized monitoring, backups, and automated deployments.

NFR-01.title: Security and Access Control
requirements: All sensitive data must be encrypted in transit and at rest
Role-based authorization must restrict access by module and action
All authentication events and privileged actions must be logged

NFR-02.title: Performance and Scalability
requirements: Core pages and dashboards should load within acceptable enterprise SLAs
The platform must support concurrent usage across multiple departments
Background jobs and reporting workloads should not block transactional workflows

NFR-03.title: Reliability and Recoverability
requirements: Automated backups must run on a defined schedule
Critical workflows should support retry-safe processing and audit logging
Operational alerts should be generated for failures and degraded services

system_attributes.reliability: Platform should provide resilient transactional processing with backup and recovery capabilities.
system_attributes.scalability: Architecture should support horizontal scaling for APIs, background workers, and reporting services.
system_attributes.security: Authentication, authorization, encryption, and auditability must be built into every module.
system_attributes.maintainability: Codebase should remain modular, observable, and safe to extend through clear service boundaries.

out_of_scope: Native mobile applications in the first release
Large third-party marketplace or legacy ERP migrations unless explicitly scoped
Non-core experimental modules outside the platform's operational workflows

FR-01.title: Policy Browsing and Comparison
requirements: The system must support policy browsing and comparison as a complete business workflow within the application.
Policy Browsing and Comparison should validate required inputs, enforce role-based permissions, and prevent invalid state changes.
Users must be able to review the current state and recent activity associated with policy browsing and comparison from the relevant module.
The platform should log significant actions, failures, and user-visible events related to policy browsing and comparison for operational support.

FR-01.title: Plan Policy Browsing and Comparison Workflow and Contracts
FR-01.description: Review the SRS requirement bullets for policy browsing and comparison and break them into executable API, UI, data, security, and reporting subtasks. Define the workflow states, user roles, field-level validations, request/response contracts, and failure cases before implementation starts. Use these requirement drivers as the baseline: The system must support policy browsing and comparison as a complete business workflow within the application; Policy Browsing and Comparison should validate required inputs, enforce role-based permissions, and prevent invalid state changes; Users must be able to review the current state and recent activity associated with policy browsing and comparison from the relevant module.
acceptance_criteria: The end-to-end workflow for policy browsing and comparison is documented from trigger to completion
Validation rules, role restrictions, and error scenarios for policy browsing and comparison are explicitly defined
Implementation subtasks for policy browsing and comparison can be picked up independently by engineering

FR-01.title: Implement Policy Browsing and Comparison Backend Services
FR-01.description: Build the backend services for policy browsing and comparison on React, Java Spring Boot, Oracle DB, RabbitMQ. Create route handlers or service endpoints, model the core business operations, enforce validation and authorization rules, persist workflow state changes, and add structured error handling and audit logging. Make sure the implementation covers these behaviors: The system must support policy browsing and comparison as a complete business workflow within the application; Policy Browsing and Comparison should validate required inputs, enforce role-based permissions, and prevent invalid state changes; Users must be able to review the current state and recent activity associated with policy browsing and comparison from the relevant module.
acceptance_criteria: Core policy browsing and comparison business rules are implemented for Insurance Management Platform
Validation, authorization, and error handling are covered for policy browsing and comparison
API/service behavior for policy browsing and comparison is testable and documented for integration

FR-01.title: Implement Policy Browsing and Comparison Frontend Flow
FR-01.description: Build the frontend flow for policy browsing and comparison with the exact forms, tables, filters, wizards, status indicators, and action buttons required by the SRS. Connect the UI to the backend contract, handle optimistic updates or refresh flows where needed, and provide user-facing validation, permissions feedback, loading states, and recovery states.
acceptance_criteria: The policy browsing and comparison user flow is accessible and responsive across supported browsers
Forms, filters, tables, and feedback states for policy browsing and comparison behave correctly
The policy browsing and comparison UI is fully integrated with backend APIs and handles loading and error states

FR-01.title: Integrate External Dependencies for Policy Browsing and Comparison
FR-01.description: Implement the external integration points used by policy browsing and comparison, such as third-party APIs, SSO, messaging gateways, payment rails, import/export pipelines, or bank feeds. Add retry-safe behavior, timeout handling, observability, and fallback behavior for downstream failures.
acceptance_criteria: External dependencies used by policy browsing and comparison are connected through stable integration layers
Timeout, retry, and failure handling for policy browsing and comparison integrations are implemented and testable
Secrets, credentials, and integration logs for policy browsing and comparison are handled securely

FR-01.title: Harden Policy Browsing and Comparison Security and Access Rules
FR-01.description: Apply security controls to policy browsing and comparison. Implement role checks, field-level protections, data masking where appropriate, secure defaults, audit trails, and abuse-prevention rules. Verify that sensitive operations are guarded both in the API layer and in the UI visibility rules.
acceptance_criteria: Access control rules for policy browsing and comparison are enforced consistently across API and UI layers
Sensitive operations and data paths in policy browsing and comparison are audited and protected against misuse
Security validation for policy browsing and comparison passes review and automated checks

FR-01.title: Write Policy Browsing and Comparison Automated and End-to-End Tests
FR-01.description: Write unit, integration, and end-to-end tests for policy browsing and comparison. Cover valid flows, invalid inputs, permission failures, state transitions, data integrity checks, and regression-prone edge cases pulled from the SRS. Add fixtures or mocks that make the feature verifiable in CI and local development.
acceptance_criteria: Happy path and failure cases for policy browsing and comparison are covered by automated tests
Permission checks and validation rules for policy browsing and comparison are verified
Regression coverage exists for the most critical policy browsing and comparison scenarios
